content.content:
{"body":"
GO:1990334   Bfa1-Bub2 complex 

would be better called
SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex

narrow synonym  Byr4-Cdc16 GAP complex
narrow_synonym  Bfa1-Bub2 complex



Also revise the definition accordingly
A protein complex that acts as a two-component GTPase-activating protein for Tem1 GTPase, thus regulating a signal transduction cascade, called the mitotic exit network (MEN), which is required for mitotic exit and cytokinesis. Bub2/Bfa1 keeps Tem1 inactive until the spindle is properly oriented, thus inhibiting MEN activation. PMID:16449187

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:1990334
name: Bfa1-Bub2 complex
namespace: cellular_component
def: "A protein complex that acts as a two-component GTPase-activating protein for Tem1 GTPase, thus regulating a signal transduction cascade, called the mitotic exit network (MEN), which is required for mitotic exit and cytokinesis. Bub2/Bfa1 keeps Tem1 inactive until the spindle is properly oriented, thus inhibiting MEN activation." [GOC:bhm, PMID:16449187]
is_a: GO:1902773 ! GTPase activator complex
relationship: part_of GO:0005816 ! spindle pole body
created_by: bhm
creation_date: 2014-03-17T14:34:21Z

[Term]
id: GO:1990334
name: Bfa1-Bub2 complex
namespace: cellular_component
def: "A protein complex that acts as a two-component GTPase-activating protein for Tem1 GTPase, thus regulating a signal transduction cascade, called the mitotic exit network (MEN), which is required for mitotic exit and cytokinesis. Bub2/Bfa1 keeps Tem1 inactive until the spindle is properly oriented, thus inhibiting MEN activation." [GOC:bhm, PMID:16449187]
is_a: GO:1902773 ! GTPase activator complex

created_by: bhm

content.text: I'll check the build status in background and meanwhile prepare the edit.

input.content:
[Term]
id: GO:1990334
name: SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex
namespace: cellular_component
def: "A protein complex that acts as a two-component GTPase-activating protein for the Tem1/Spg1 GTPase, thus regulating a signal transduction cascade (the mitotic exit network, MEN, in budding yeast; the septation initiation network, SIN, in fission yeast), which is required for mitotic exit and cytokinesis. The complex keeps the GTPase inactive until the spindle is properly oriented, thus inhibiting MEN/SIN activation." [GOC:bhm, PMID:16449187]
synonym: "Bfa1-Bub2 complex" NARROW []
synonym: "Byr4-Cdc16 GAP complex" NARROW []
is_a: GO:1902773 ! GTPase activator complex
relationship: part_of GO:0005816 ! spindle pole body
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31636" xsd:anyURI
created_by: bhm
creation_date: 2014-03-17T14:34:21Z

diff --git a/src/ontology/go-edit.obo b/src/ontology/go-edit.obo
index abffd33..859cb9e 100644
--- a/src/ontology/go-edit.obo
+++ b/src/ontology/go-edit.obo
@@ -593152,11 +593152,14 @@ creation_date: 2014-03-17T14:11:35Z
 
 [Term]
 id: GO:1990334
-name: Bfa1-Bub2 complex
+name: SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex
 namespace: cellular_component
-def: "A protein complex that acts as a two-component GTPase-activating protein for Tem1 GTPase, thus regulating a signal transduction cascade, called the mitotic exit network (MEN), which is required for mitotic exit and cytokinesis. Bub2/Bfa1 keeps Tem1 inactive until the spindle is properly oriented, thus inhibiting MEN activation." [GOC:bhm, PMID:16449187]
+def: "A protein complex that acts as a two-component GTPase-activating protein for the Tem1/Spg1 GTPase, thus regulating a signal transduction cascade (the mitotic exit network, MEN, in budding yeast; the septation initiation network, SIN, in fission yeast), which is required for mitotic exit and cytokinesis. The complex keeps the GTPase inactive until the spindle is properly oriented, thus inhibiting MEN/SIN activation." [GOC:bhm, PMID:16449187]
+synonym: "Bfa1-Bub2 complex" NARROW []
+synonym: "Byr4-Cdc16 GAP complex" NARROW []
 is_a: GO:1902773 ! GTPase activator complex
 relationship: part_of GO:0005816 ! spindle pole body
+property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31636" xsd:anyURI
 created_by: bhm
 creation_date: 2014-03-17T14:34:21Z

input.command:
git add src/ontology/go-edit.obo && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Rename GO:1990334 to species-agnostic label 'SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex'

Addresses geneontology/go-ontology#31636.

- Name: 'Bfa1-Bub2 complex' -> 'SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex'
- Added NARROW synonyms: 'Bfa1-Bub2 complex' (budding yeast) and
  'Byr4-Cdc16 GAP complex' (fission yeast)
- Updated definition to reference both MEN (S. cerevisiae) and SIN
  (S. pombe) signalling cascades and the Tem1/Spg1 GTPase, so it matches
  the species-agnostic label.
- Added term_tracker_item pointing at the issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Rename GO:1990334 Bfa1-Bub2 complex -> 'SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex'" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
Renames GO:1990334 to a species-agnostic label and adds yeast-specific narrow synonyms, per request in #31636.

- **name**: `Bfa1-Bub2 complex` → `SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex`
- **synonym (NARROW)**: `Bfa1-Bub2 complex` (S. cerevisiae)
- **synonym (NARROW)**: `Byr4-Cdc16 GAP complex` (S. pombe)
- **def**: updated so it matches the species-agnostic label, referencing both the mitotic exit network (MEN, budding yeast) and septation initiation network (SIN, fission yeast), and the Tem1/Spg1 GTPase. Reference PMID:16449187 retained.
- **property_value**: `term_tracker_item` → issue #31636 added.

Note on the definition: the issue listed a revised definition that was textually identical to the existing one. Since the whole point of the rename is species-agnosticism, I made a minimal edit to bring the definition wording in line with the new label (explicitly mentioning both SIN and MEN, and the Tem1/Spg1 GTPase). Happy to revert to the original wording if @ValWood prefers.

## Checklist
- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION — `make travis_build` passed pre-edit
- [x] RESEARCH — N/A (label change per curator request; PMID:16449187 already present)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH — confirmed only one matching stanza via `obo-grep.pl`
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS — N/A (no compositional axiom changes)
- [x] EDITS — used `obo-checkout.pl` / `obo-checkin.pl`
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS — `is_a GO:1902773` and `part_of GO:0005816` unchanged
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS — N/A
- [x] METADATA — `created_by` / `creation_date` untouched (existing term); added `term_tracker_item`
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION — `make travis_build` passed post-edit (all SPARQL-QC rules PASS, reasoning OK)
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — no new PMIDs; existing PMID:16449187 retained
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED — single commit touching only `src/ontology/go-edit.obo`

## Test plan
- [x] `cd src/ontology && make travis_build` completes without violations

Closes #31636

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @ValWood
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24660028429)
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh issue comment 31636 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Renamed **GO:1990334** from `Bfa1-Bub2 complex` to **`SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex`** and added the two yeast-specific complexes as `NARROW` synonyms:

- `Bfa1-Bub2 complex` NARROW (S. cerevisiae)
- `Byr4-Cdc16 GAP complex` NARROW (S. pombe)

The definition you provided was textually identical to the existing one, so — since the point of the rename is species-agnosticism — I took the liberty of tweaking the wording so the definition matches the new label (explicitly referencing both MEN and SIN, and the Tem1/Spg1 GTPase). PMID:16449187 retained.

New def:
> A protein complex that acts as a two-component GTPase-activating protein for the Tem1/Spg1 GTPase, thus regulating a signal transduction cascade (the mitotic exit network, MEN, in budding yeast; the septation initiation network, SIN, in fission yeast), which is required for mitotic exit and cytokinesis. The complex keeps the GTPase inactive until the spindle is properly oriented, thus inhibiting MEN/SIN activation. [GOC:bhm, PMID:16449187]

If you'd prefer to keep the original definition verbatim, let me know and I'll revert that part.

PR: #31925 — `make travis_build` passes.

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @ValWood
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24660028429)
EOF
)"

Renamed GO:1990334 to `SIN/MEN two-component GAP complex`, added `Bfa1-Bub2 complex` and `Byr4-Cdc16 GAP complex` as NARROW synonyms, and updated the definition to reference both MEN (S. cerevisiae) and SIN (S. pombe) plus Tem1/Spg1. `make travis_build` passes. PR #31925 opened; issue #31636 updated with a summary and a note flagging the definition tweak for @ValWood's review.
